title: Beat Saber Troubleshooter
intro: Pick the step you're stuck on and follow the questions. If nothing here solves it, contact support at the bottom of any result.
supportChecklist: Screenshot of the error or what you see

root.title: What step are you stuck on?
root.question: Choose the stage that best matches your problem.
root.details: If you are not sure, start at the beginning — Downloading APKs — and work forward.

next.title: Downloading APKs
next.question: Are you missing APKs or are they not downloading at all?
next.details: Go to https://bsmd.sm0ke.org/ in your Quest browser and tap Download All.

choices.label: APKs are missing from the page

result.title: Accept the download permission
result.summary: Your Quest browser blocked the downloads. You need to allow multiple downloads.
steps: Open https://bsmd.sm0ke.org/ in your Quest browser.
Tap Download All.
When the banner appears at the bottom asking to allow multiple downloads, tap Accept or Allow.
Wait for all three APKs to finish downloading.

result.title: Check your connection
result.summary: Slow or unstable Wi-Fi is the most common cause of stalled downloads.
steps: Make sure your Quest is connected to Wi-Fi and has a strong signal.
Move closer to your router if the signal is weak.
Try downloading one APK at a time instead of all at once.
If your school or work network blocks downloads, try a different network or a mobile hotspot.

choices.label: Installing APKs
choices.description: Can't install or open the APK files

next.title: Installing APKs
next.question: What's going wrong?
next.details: APKs live in the Files app → Downloads folder. Tap the ... next to each APK and choose Package installer.

choices.label: I can't find the APKs in the Files app

result.title: Accept the download permissions
result.summary: The APKs may not have fully downloaded, or the browser blocked the batch download.
steps: Open https://bsmd.sm0ke.org/ in your Quest browser.
Tap Download All.
If a banner appears at the bottom of the browser asking to allow multiple downloads, tap Accept.
Wait for all downloads to complete, then open the Files app and tap Downloads.

choices.label: I used MVRS and it's not working

result.title: Use the default Files app instead
result.summary: Mobile VR Station (MVRS) is no longer needed. The built-in Files app handles APK installs directly.
steps: Open the Files app (pre-installed on every Quest).
Tap This Headset → Downloads.
Tap the ... next to the first APK and choose Open with.
Select Package installer.
Tap Settings → allow installs from unknown sources → go back and tap Install.
Tap Done (do not open the app yet).
Repeat for each remaining APK.

choices.label: The APK won't install at all

next.title: APK won't install
next.question: What do you see when you try to install?

result.title: Allow installs from unknown sources
result.summary: Android blocks APKs from outside the store by default. You need to allow it once.
steps: Tap the ... next to the APK in Files → Downloads.
Choose Open with → Package installer.
Tap Settings on the installer screen.
Enable Allow from this source.
Press the back button and tap Install.

result.title: Get support
result.summary: Take a screenshot of exactly what you see and bring it to support.

choices.label: Enabling Developer Mode
choices.description: Build Number not working, Wireless Debugging issues

next.title: Enabling Developer Mode and Wireless Debugging
next.question: What issue are you experiencing?
next.details: Developer mode is enabled by tapping Build Number 7 times in Settings → About Headset.

choices.label: I tapped Build Number many times but nothing happened

next.title: Build Number not responding
next.question: Do you have Multiple Accounts enabled on this headset?
next.details: Go to Settings → Accounts and check whether there is more than one account listed.

result.title: Switch to the admin account
result.summary: Developer mode can only be enabled from the primary admin account. Guest and secondary accounts cannot turn it on.
steps: Switch to the primary (admin) account on the headset.
Open Settings → About Headset.
Tap Build Number 7 times until you see "You are now a developer".
Go back to Settings → Developer options to confirm it's listed there.

next.title: Restart your headset and try again
next.question: Hold the power button, tap Show more, then tap Restart. After the headset comes back on, try tapping Build Number again.

result.title: Keep going!
result.summary: Developer mode is now on. Head back to the guide and continue with Wireless Debugging.

next.title: Wait 1–2 hours and try again
next.question: Meta occasionally rate-limits developer mode activation. Waiting and retrying usually fixes it.

result.title: Keep going!
result.summary: Developer mode is now on. Head back to the guide and continue.

result.title: Get support
result.summary: Please ask in the Discord or Support Chat. Include your headset model and what you see when you tap Build Number.

choices.label: Wireless Debugging won't turn on or I can't find it

next.title: Wireless Debugging
next.question: What do you see?
next.details: Wireless Debugging is inside Settings → Developer options. Developer mode must be on first.

choices.label: I can't find Developer options at all

result.title: Enable developer mode first
result.summary: Developer options only appears after you've tapped Build Number 7 times.
steps: Open Dev Tools Opener and tap Open Settings → Open.
Go to About Headset.
Tap Build Number 7 times until you see "You are now a developer".
Go back to the main Settings screen — Developer options should now appear at the bottom.

choices.label: Developer options is there but Wireless Debugging is greyed out

result.title: Connect to Wi-Fi first
result.summary: Wireless Debugging requires an active Wi-Fi connection. It will stay greyed out if you're not connected.
steps: Open Settings → Wi-Fi and connect to your network.
Go back to Developer options and toggle Wireless Debugging on.

choices.label: Wireless Debugging is on but pairing fails

result.title: Pairing tips
result.summary: The pairing code expires quickly — you only have about 60 seconds to enter it.
steps: Inside the Wireless Debugging menu, tap Pair device with pairing code.
Immediately enter the 6-digit code shown into MBF Launcher — do not wait.
If it says pairing failed, tap Pair device with pairing code again to get a fresh code and try once more.
Make sure your phone/PC and Quest are on the same Wi-Fi network.

choices.label: Expected mods are missing from the list

result.title: Check your Beat Saber version
result.summary: Not all mods are available for every version. The mod list you see depends on which version of Beat Saber is installed.
steps: Check which version you are on — it shows at the bottom of the Beat Saber main menu.
Some mods are only available on 1.37, others only on 1.40.8.
If you need a specific mod, you may need to change version.

result.title: Patches are being prepared — please wait
result.summary: When Beat Saber updates, the MBF team has to generate new mod patches. This is normal and usually takes between 1 hour and 2 days.
steps: Do not try to force-install mods while this message is showing.
Check back in a few hours.
You can watch the MBF GitHub or the Discord for announcements on when patches are ready.

choices.label: MBF Launcher won't connect / ADB error

result.title: Reconnect your headset
result.summary: MBF Launcher communicates with Beat Saber over ADB (Wireless Debugging). If the connection drops, you need to re-pair.
steps: Make sure Wireless Debugging is still on in your headset's Developer options.
Close and reopen MBF Launcher.
If it still fails, tap Pair device with pairing code inside Wireless Debugging and enter the new code into MBF Launcher.
Try using MBF Tools if you have it — it can reconnect automatically.

result.title: Get support
result.summary: Bring a screenshot of the error to support.

choices.label: Game Crash or won't load
choices.description: Crashes on launch or while playing

next.title: Game Crash or won't load
next.question: What version are you on?
next.details: Your version is shown on your feat in the Beat Saber menu.

choices.label: Crashes on launch (before getting to the menu)

next.title: Crash on launch — 1.37
next.question: Have you tried closing and reopening Beat Saber?
next.details: First crashes after modding are normal — the game sometimes needs 2–3 attempts to load for the first time.

result.title: Have fun!
result.summary: First-launch crashes after modding are normal. Beat Saber should load reliably after the first successful boot.

result.title: Get crash logs and ask for help
result.summary: Install Crash Reporter to capture logs, then bring the username to support so they can diagnose the crash.
steps: Go to https://bsquest.xyz/crgen/crgen.html and enter your Beat Saber username.
Download and install the generated crash reporter config.
Try launching Beat Saber again to capture the crash.
Post your username in Discord or the Support Chat so support can look at the logs.

next.title: Song crash — 1.37
next.question: Does it crash on every song or only specific ones?

result.title: The map may need mods you don't have
result.summary: Some custom maps require Chroma, Noodle Extensions, or Mapping Extensions. If those are missing the game can crash.
steps: Check the map's page on BeatSaver — it will list any required mods.
Install any missing mods through MBF Launcher.
Try the song again.

choices.label: Crashes on most or all songs

result.title: Remove known problem mods
result.summary: A few mods are known to cause song crashes on 1.37. Try removing them first.
steps: Open MBF Launcher and go to Your Mods.
Uninstall IForgor, Endless, and Qosmetics if they are installed.
If that doesn't help, install Crash Reporter and bring your username to support.

choices.label: Crashes on launch (before getting to the menu)

next.title: Crash on launch — 1.40.8
next.question: Have you tried closing and reopening Beat Saber?
next.details: The game sometimes needs 2–3 attempts to load after modding.

result.title: Have fun!
result.summary: First-launch crashes after modding are normal and should stop after the first successful boot.

result.title: Get crash logs and ask for help
result.summary: Install Crash Reporter and bring your username to support.
steps: Go to https://bsquest.xyz/crgen/crgen.html and enter your Beat Saber username.
Download and install the generated crash reporter config.
Try launching Beat Saber again to capture the crash.
Post your username in Discord or the Support Chat.

result.title: Get crash logs and ask for help
result.summary: Song crashes on 1.40.8 usually need a log to diagnose. Install Crash Reporter and bring your username to support.
steps: Go to https://bsquest.xyz/crgen/crgen.html and enter your Beat Saber username.
Download and install the generated crash reporter config.
Play the song that crashes to capture the log.
Post your username in Discord or Support Chat.

choices.label: Mods or custom songs not showing
choices.description: Missing mods after modding, songs not loading

next.title: Mods or custom songs not showing
next.question: What's missing?

choices.label: Mods are gone after I updated Beat Saber

result.title: Mods are wiped by updates — you need to remod
result.summary: Every time Beat Saber updates it replaces the game files and removes all mods. This is expected.
steps: Open MBF Launcher.
It will detect that the game is now unmodded and offer to patch it again.
Select your version and tap Mod the App.
Reinstall your mods from the mod list.

choices.label: Custom songs are missing or the Custom Levels menu isn't there

result.title: Make sure Better Song Search is installed
result.summary: Custom songs are loaded by SongCore and browsed with Better Song Search. Both need to be installed.
steps: Open MBF Launcher and check that Better Song Search is in Your Mods.
If it's missing, go to Add Mods, find Better Song Search, and install it.
Launch Beat Saber — the Custom Levels menu should now appear in Solo mode.
Use Better Song Search to download songs from inside the game.

choices.label: A specific mod isn't showing up in-game

result.title: Check the mod is correctly installed
result.summary: If a mod installs without errors but doesn't appear in-game, it may not be compatible with your version.
steps: Open MBF Launcher → Your Mods and confirm the mod shows as installed (not just listed).
Check you are on the version the mod supports — some mods are 1.37-only or 1.40.8-only.
Try restarting Beat Saber.
If the mod has a settings menu, look for it in the game's Mod Settings screen (⚙️ → Mod Settings).

choices.label: A map says it requires Vivify

result.title: Vivify is not available on Quest yet
result.summary: Vivify is a PC-only mod that is currently in development for Quest. There is no release date. Maps that require Vivify cannot be played on Quest right now.
steps: Check the map's BeatSaver page — if Vivify is listed as a requirement, the map is PC-only for now.
There is no workaround. The map will not load correctly without Vivify.
Keep an eye on the Discord for announcements when Vivify for Quest becomes available.

result.title: Ask for support
result.summary: If your problem isn't covered here, bring it to the Discord or Support Chat. Include your headset model, Beat Saber version, and a screenshot of what you see.
